articles.title: MISTRAL SABA
articles.summary: Mistral Saba is a 24B parameter model trained on meticulously curated datasets from across the Middle East and South Asia. The model provides more accurate and relevant responses than models that are over 5 times its size while being significantly faster and lower cost.

articles.title: SOUTH KOREA BANS DEEPSEEK
articles.summary: South Korea has temporarily banned new downloads of China's DeepSeek AI chatbot due to data protection concerns. The app, which gained over a million users in a week, will remain unavailable until compliance improvements are made.

articles.title: FAL RAISES $49M SERIES B TO POWER THE FUTURE OF AI VIDEO
articles.summary: fal has secured $49M in Series B funding led by Notable Capital and joined by a16z and others, totaling $72M in funding to date. The company is focused on expanding its platform for AI-driven generative media, especially video content, catering to industries like advertising and gaming. fal's proprietary technology promises fast, scalable, and reliable deployments, which has already attracted enterprise clients such as Quora and Canva.

articles.title: LARGE DIFFUSION LANGUAGE MODEL
articles.summary: Large scale training of a diffusion model for language that matches LLaMA 3 8B in performance across many benchmarks.

articles.title: EVALUATING LLMS WITHOUT HUMAN JUDGMENT
articles.summary: Researchers introduce a benchmark that assesses LLM open-ended text generation using n-gram statistics and rules, avoiding reliance on human or LLM-based judgments. It closely correlates with GPT-4o evaluations while being computationally efficient.

articles.title: SPEEDING UP LLM INFERENCE WITH COPYSPEC
articles.summary: CopySpec is a method that accelerates LLM inference by identifying and copying repeated sequences in chat history without extra GPU memory usage. It achieves up to 3.08x speed-up on specific tasks and integrates effectively with speculative decoding for additional gains.

articles.title: STEP AUDIO CHAT (HUGGING FACE HUB)
articles.summary: This is the Multimodal Large Language Model (LLM) component of Step-Audio. It is a 130-billion-parameter multimodal LLM that is responsible for understanding and generating human speech. The model is specifically designed to seamlessly integrate functions such as speech recognition, semantic understanding, dialogue management, voice cloning, and speech generation.

articles.title: MEASURING LLMS MEMORY
articles.summary: This study explores the limitations of existing methods for evaluating the memory capacity of language models. It introduces the "forgetting curve," a new approach for assessing how well models retain information over long contexts.

articles.title: INDOOR NAVIGATION
articles.summary: AdaSimulator provides a physics-enabled environment for studying Visual Language Navigation (VLN) in realistic settings.

articles.title: US' FIRST MAJOR AI COPYRIGHT RULING
articles.summary: A U.S. judge ruled that Ross Intelligence infringed on Thomson Reuters' copyright by using Westlaw headnotes to train its AI. The decision may influence other AI-related copyright lawsuits but remains narrowly focused on non-generative AI applications.

articles.title: RED HAT'S TAKE ON OPEN-SOURCE AI: PRAGMATISM OVER UTOPIAN DREAMS
articles.summary: Red Hat emphasizes a pragmatic approach to open-source AI, focusing on practical enterprise needs rather than chasing AGI. Challenges include reconciling transparency with competitive realities, especially regarding open-source AI's opacity in training data and model weights. Red Hat aims to foster collaboration, avoiding vendor lock-in, while acknowledging the complexities of AI compared to traditional open-source software.

articles.title: THE EU AI ACT IS COMING TO AMERICA
articles.summary: Although federal leadership seems wary of heavy AI regulations, many US states are implementing legislation modeled on Europe's AI Act. This article outlines how “algorithmic discrimination” laws, inspired by EU rules, could create detailed impact assessments, require compliance paperwork, and impose liability on AI deployments—raising the real prospect of higher operating costs for teams building AI systems.

articles.title: THIS PROJECT REQUIRED ZERO MEETINGS—AND IT'S NOT BECAUSE AI DID ALL THE WORK
articles.summary: Andrew Boyagi of Atlassian went viral when he posted about running a project from start to finish with zero meetings. Want a behind the

articles.title: scenes look at how it went down? Watch the video
CHATGPT COMES TO 500,000 NEW USERS IN OPENAI'S LARGEST AI EDUCATION DEAL YET
articles.summary: OpenAI is deploying ChatGPT to 460,000 students and 63,000 faculty at California State University for personalized tutoring and administrative tasks.

articles.title: TINDER WILL TRY AI-POWERED MATCHING AS THE DATING APP CONTINUES TO LOSE USERS
articles.summary: Tinder plans to counter declining user engagement by introducing AI-driven features for discovery and matching.

articles.title: GOOGLE IS ADDING DIGITAL WATERMARKS TO IMAGES EDITED WITH MAGIC EDITOR AI
articles.summary: Google is introducing a digital watermark in Photos for images edited with generative AI using the Magic Editor's Reimagine feature on Pixel 9 devices.
